title: Athyrium rubricaule
intro: Athyrium rubricaule, also known as autumn fern, is a species of fern that is native to China and Japan. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium rubricaule include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Athyrium rubricaule is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Athyrium rubricaule is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a member of the genus Athyrium, which consists of several species of ferns. The genus Athyrium is further classified as belonging to the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns with two distinct types of leaves. The order Polypodiales is part of the class Polypodiopsida, a large and diverse group of ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is then classified as part of the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns and fern-like plants.
